ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the weather station */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the weather station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the weather station */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the station was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "sensor_types" TEXT /* Types of sensors installed at the station */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the station (e.g., active, maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather event */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather event */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather event */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the weather event */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the weather event */,
  "impact" TEXT /* Impact of the weather event (e.g., minor, moderate, severe) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, data analyst, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team the user belongs to */,
  "joined_date" TEXT /* Date when the user joined */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the weather data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the weather data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the browser or client used */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data file */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data entry the file belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the data file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the data file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the file (e.g., CSV, JSON, XML) */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the file (e.g., comma-separated, tab-separated) */,
  "file_checksum" TEXT /* Checksum of the file for integrity verification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "readme_files" (
  "readme_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each readme file */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data entry the readme file belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the readme file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the readme file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the file (e.g., text, markdown) */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the file (e.g., plain text, markdown) */,
  "file_checksum" TEXT /* Checksum of the file for integrity verification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("readme_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data version */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data entry */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the data entry */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "changes" TEXT /* Description of changes made in this version */,
  "version_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the version */,
  "version_reason" TEXT /* Reason for creating the version */,
  "version_status" TEXT /* Status of the version (e.g., draft, approved, deprecated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_version_by FOREIGN KEY ("version_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality" (
  "quality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality metric entry */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data entry */,
  "temperature_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the temperature data is valid */,
  "humidity_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the humidity data is valid */,
  "pressure_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the pressure data is valid */,
  "visibility_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the visibility data is valid */,
  "wind_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the wind speed data is valid */,
  "precipitation_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the precipitation data is valid */,
  "cloud_cover_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the cloud cover data is valid */,
  "event_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the weather event data is valid */,
  "wind_dir_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the wind direction data is valid */,
  "quality_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the quality check */,
  "quality_date" TEXT /* Date when the quality check was performed */,
  "quality_comments" TEXT /* Comments or notes from the quality check */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quality_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_quality_by FOREIGN KEY ("quality_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_anomalies" (
  "anomaly_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each anomaly entry */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data entry where the anomaly was detected */,
  "anomaly_type" TEXT /* Type of anomaly (e.g., temperature spike, pressure drop) */,
  "anomaly_date" TEXT /* Date when the anomaly was detected */,
  "anomaly_description" TEXT /* Description of the anomaly */,
  "anomaly_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who detected the anomaly */,
  "anomaly_status" TEXT /* Status of the anomaly (e.g., uninvestigated, under investigation, resolved) */,
  "anomaly_resolution" TEXT /* Resolution or action taken for the anomaly */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("anomaly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_anomalies_anomaly_by FOREIGN KEY ("anomaly_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
